name: Poppy Seed Kolaches
recipeInstructions:
1. In a mixing bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water. Add the next 7 ingredients and 2 cups of flour; beat until smooth. Add enough remaining flour to form a soft dough.
2. Turn dough onto a floured board; knead until smooth and elastic, about 6-8 minutes. Place in a greased bowl, turning once to grease the top. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
3. Punch dough down. Shape into walnut-sized balls; roll each into a 2-1/2-inch circle. Place 2 inches apart on greased baking sheets; brush with butter. Cover and let rise until doubled, about 30 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, in a saucepan, bring poppy seeds and water to a boil; boil 1 minute or until thickened. Add milk and raisins; simmer for 10 minutes. Add sugar, butter, vanilla, and cinnamon; simmer for 5 minutes. Remove from the heat; stir in crumbs.
5. Make a depression about 1-1/2 inches in diameter in the center of each roll. Fill with 2 teaspoons of filling.
6. Bake at 400°F for 7-10 minutes or until golden brown. Cool on wire racks. Drizzle with glaze if desired.
